content.problemStatement:
You are running a game where kids pick balls labeled with both positive and negative numbers. Your goal is to calculate the total sum of the numbers on three selected balls.

**Ball Values**

• Ball 1: 20

• Ball 2: -3

• Ball 3: 5
constraints: The program performs only constant‑time arithmetic.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer – the sum of the three ball values (20, -3 and 5).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three fixed ball values are 20, -3 and 5. Their sum is 22, which is printed.
Even if the program internally loops over a single test case, the result remains 22.
No matter how many times the loop runs (here once), the constant sum is 22.

solution.approach: The problem asks for the sum of three fixed integers: 20, -3 and 5. The optimal algorithm is a direct constant‑time addition of these three numbers. Even though the statement mentions handling T test cases, there is no input, so we assume a single test case (T = 1). The program computes sum = 20 + (-3) + 5 and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – only a few arithmetic operations are performed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
